info.title: Dexploit API
info.description: Real-time and historical Solana DEX swap and OHLCV data.

BearerAuth.description: Required by the OHLCV endpoints (`/api/v1/*`) and accepted by every other endpoint. Send `Authorization: Bearer ohlcv_live_sk_<your_key>`.

ApiKeyQuery.name: api_key
ApiKeyQuery.description: Browser-friendly alternative to the Bearer header — accepted by every endpoint and required for WebSocket from the browser.

message.example: use pair_address; see /api/v1/pairs?token_address=X to discover pools

recent_volume_sol.description: Volume in SOL over a recent rolling window

base_mint.description: Base side of the pool — almost always wrapped SOL.

volume_sol.description: **Lamports**, despite the field name. Divide by 1e9 to get SOL.

volume_token.description: Token base units. Divide by 10**`quote_decimals` for human-readable.

DexId.description:
Integer DEX identifier used in raw swap rows. Mapping:

| ID | DEX |
| -- | --- |
| 1  | `pumpfun` |
| 2  | `pumpswap` |
| 3  | `raydium_amm` |
| 4  | `raydium_clmm` |
| 5  | `raydium_cpmm` |
| 6  | `orca` |
| 7  | `meteora_damm_v2` |
| 8  | `meteora_dbc` |
| 9  | `meteora_dlmm` |
| 10 | `meteora_pools` |

Swap.description:
A single swap event from the raw swaps index. Same shape across `/swaps`, `/swaps/range`, `/swaps/whale`, `/swaps/{signature}`, `/swaps/trader/{wallet}`, `/swaps/token/{mint}`.

Note that the streaming feeds (WebSocket and gRPC) use a *different* shape — see [WebSocket wire format](/streaming/websocket) — most notably `swap_type` (string) vs `is_buy` (bool) here, and `dex` as the string name vs `dex` as the integer ID here.

token_address.description: Non-SOL side of the pool (the token being bought/sold).

is_buy.description: `true` if SOL → token; `false` if token → SOL.

token_amount.description: Token base units; divide by 10**`quote_decimals` for human-readable.

fee_bps.description: Effective LP fee, in basis points.

creator_fee.description: Creator-set fee paid, in lamports (pump.fun + meteora-dbc).

price_per_token.description: Realized fill price (SOL per 1 token, decimals-adjusted).

price_impact_bps.description: Difference between fill price and pre-swap mid, in basis points.

virtual_sol_reserves.description: Virtual SOL reserves (pump.fun bonding curve). 0 on non-pump pools.

real_sol_reserves.description: Actual reserves on the pool after the swap.

transfer_fee_in.description: Token-2022 transfer-fee paid on the input side (lamports/base units).

SwapsPage.description: Paged list of swaps, shared by `/swaps`, `/swaps/range`, `/swaps/whale`.

total.description: Count of swaps in this page.

has_more.description: True if more rows match — increment `offset` to page.

GlobalStats.description: Lifetime totals across the entire index (not a 24h window).

TraderStats.description: Lifetime stats for a wallet across all pools and DEXs.

total_sol_volume.description: Cumulative SOL volume routed by this wallet.

unique_tokens.description: Number of distinct token mints this wallet has traded.

TokenStats.description: Lifetime stats for a token mint across every indexed pool.

TopMover.description: One pool in a top-movers response. The `gainers` and `losers` arrays each contain these.

mint.description: Token mint (non-SOL side of the pool).

volume_sol.description: SOL volume over `timeframe`. **Lamports** despite the name — divide by 1e9.

TrendingResult.description: One token in a /trending response. Volume and liquidity are in USD via the Pyth SOL/USD oracle.

change_pct.description: Price change percent over the trending `window`. `null` when the window contains only one swap or the start price was zero.

trending_score.description: Composite score (only present when `include_score=true`). Higher = more momentum.

formula_breakdown.description: Per-component contribution to `trending_score`. Only present when `include_score=true`. All components sum to `trending_score`.

vol_acceleration.description: `ln(vol_now / vol_prior) * 4`, clamped to `[-8, 12]`. Brand-new mints with no prior-window activity get a fixed `+4` boost so first-window tokens can surface. This is the dominant momentum term.

buyer_acceleration.description: `((buyers_now - buyers_prior) / max(buyers_prior, 1)) * 2`, clamped to `[-2, 6]`. Percent-change in unique buyers vs the prior window.

dev_sell_penalty.description: Negative. `-min(dev_sell_sol * 0.5, 20)` — capped subtraction per SOL sold by the token's creator.

holder_growth.description: Reserved for future use. Always `null` today.

top10_penalty.description: Reserved for future use. Always `null` today.

change_pct_by_window.description: Populated only when `extra_windows=...` was set on the request. Keys are the window strings you asked for (e.g. `"5m"`, `"24h"`); values are the price change percent for that window, or `null` if no price reference at window start.

PoolEvent.description: Liquidity-pool event other than a swap — LP deposit, LP withdraw, or token burn.

lp_token_amount.description: DAMM v2 saturates this to u64::MAX — prefer `base_amount` + `quote_amount` for math.

ScreenerFilters.description: Body filters for POST /screener. All fields optional; combine as AND.

change_pct.description: Price-change range over the screener window.

audit.description: Require audit flags — e.g. `{ "lp_burnt": true, "mint_revoked": true }`.

sectors.description: Only return tokens in any of these sector tags.

last_ts.description: Epoch ms of most recent swap considered.

first_ts.description: Epoch ms of first swap in the window.

TokenAudit.description: On-chain authority and LP-burn checks for a token.

lp_burnt.description: True if the dominant LP token is burnt/locked.

top10_pct.description: Share of supply held by top 10 wallets (excluding the pool).

TokenMetadata.description: Token metadata as indexed from on-chain + the Metaplex URI. Audit flags are inlined.

image_url_cached.description: Dexploit-hosted mirror; safe to embed without CORS pain.

supply_atomic.description: Base-unit supply; divide by 10**decimals for human-readable.

TokenIntelEntry.description: One entry in the `snipers`, `insiders`, `top_70`, or `dev` arrays.

TokenIntel.description: Aggregated intelligence for one token mint: actor classification, holders, price/volume trend, dev concentration. Cached server-side ~60s.

top_70.description: Top 70 holders (excluding the pool LP account).

dev.description: Creator wallet's current position, or null if unknown.

change_pct.description: Price change percent across canonical windows.

bonding_pct.description: Pump.fun bonding-curve progress (0–1); null for graduated/non-pump tokens.

creator_held_pct.description: Percent of supply still held by the creator wallet.

dev_held_lp_pct.description: Percent of LP tokens still held by the creator (when LP is not burnt).

computed_at.description: Unix epoch seconds; 0 if never computed.

WalletTagCounts.description: Per-tag count of wallets currently holding a given token. Useful for surfacing 'sniper-heavy' or 'whale-heavy' tokens.

PumpLifecycleEvent.description: A point in a pump.fun token's lifecycle: mint, bonding-curve progress, or migration to pumpswap.

event_type.description: `graduated` fires once the bonding curve completes and the token migrates to a tradeable AMM pool.

get.description:
List on-chain pools indexed by Dexploit. **Response shape varies with input:**

- **No `token_address`** (or only `protocol`): `data` is a flat array of `pair_address` strings — the cheapest way to enumerate pools.
- **With `token_address`** (or `token_addresses`): `data` is an array of `Pair` objects with `protocol` and `recent_volume_sol`, sorted by recent SOL volume — the discovery path for the `pair_address` you'll pass to candle and swap endpoints.

parameters.name: token_address
parameters.description: Token mint to find pools for. When set, the response is sorted by recent SOL volume.

parameters.name: token_addresses
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of token mints (max 200). Same enriched response as `token_address`.

200.description: List of pools. Shape depends on whether `token_address`/`token_addresses` was supplied (see endpoint description).

get.description:
Returns OHLCV bars for `pair_address` between `start` and `end`. Bars are built from real swap events — see [Swap-event OHLCV](/concepts/swap-event-ohlcv).

**Requires `pair_address`.** Sending `mint`, `pair`, or `protocol` returns 400 with a pointer to /api/v1/pairs.

400.description: Missing pair_address or sent legacy mint/pair/protocol

error.message: use pair_address; see /api/v1/pairs?token_address=X to discover pools

get.summary: Most recent candles for a pool
get.description: Returns the most-recent N candles. Same `pair_address` requirement as /api/v1/candles.
Returns recent swaps with optional filters. Use this for a real-time-ish polling view; for true real-time, use [WebSocket](/streaming/websocket) or [gRPC](/streaming/grpc).

parameters.name: min_sol
parameters.description: Minimum SOL amount (decimal SOL, not lamports).

parameters.name: signature
parameters.description: Restrict to one Solana tx signature.

get.summary: Swaps in a time or slot range
get.description: Page through historical swaps within a `[start_time, end_time]` (Unix epoch **seconds**) or `[start_slot, end_slot]` range. Combine with the same filters as [/swaps](#tag/swaps/get/swaps).
Returns recent swaps with `sol_amount` above a threshold (default 10 SOL).
Look up the swap event emitted by a specific Solana transaction. Returns the swap object directly (no envelope).

get.summary: Swap history for a trader (with lifetime stats)
get.description: All swaps a wallet has made, across pools and DEXs, plus the same `TraderStats` block returned by [/stats/trader/{wallet}](#tag/stats-trends/get/stats/trader/{wallet}) — saves a second round-trip when rendering a wallet page.

get.summary: Swap history for a token (with lifetime stats)
get.description: Returns swaps for a token across **every** pool we index, plus a `TokenStats` block. This is one of the few endpoints where token mint (not `pair_address`) is the right input — it intentionally aggregates. See [Pairs vs tokens](/concepts/pairs-vs-tokens).
Aggregate lifetime stats across the entire index. (Not a 24h window — these are cumulative since `data_inception` reported by [/api/v1/stats](#tag/stats-trends/get/api/v1/stats).)
Lifetime stats for a single wallet. Returns the `TraderStats` block directly.

Aggregate lifetime stats for a token mint across every indexed pool. Returns the `TokenStats` block directly. One of the few endpoints where token mint is correct (see [Pairs vs tokens](/concepts/pairs-vs-tokens)).

Tokens with the highest momentum over the requested window. The score compares the current window to the prior equal-length window, so a token that suddenly accelerates ranks above a steady high-volume token. Brand-new mints with no prior-window activity get a fast-riser boost. Pass `include_score=true` to expose the per-component `formula_breakdown` so you can re-sort client-side.

**Rug filter:** tokens whose price collapsed by 90% or more inside the window are excluded by default — their activity is rug-watchers piling in, not organic demand. Set `include_rugged=true` to opt in.

**Pagination:** use `offset` + `limit` to page through the scored list. The response carries `total` (how many candidates were scored before slicing), `offset`, and `limit` so you can detect end-of-list. Pricing/liquidity are USD via the Pyth SOL/USD oracle.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Page size — number of results to return.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Page offset — skip the first N scored results. Combined with `limit` for stable-cache-window pagination.

parameters.name: include_rugged
parameters.description: Include tokens whose price dropped by 90% or more inside the window. Default `false`.

parameters.name: extra_windows
parameters.description: Comma-separated list (max 3) of additional windows to populate `change_pct_by_window` on each result (e.g. `5m,24h`). Each extra window is one parallel ClickHouse query, so don't ask for more than you'll use.

total.description: Scored candidates before pagination slicing. When `offset + limit >= total`, you've reached the end.

offset.description: Echo of the effective `offset` (0 if unset).

get.description: Pools with the largest absolute price change over the requested timeframe. Returns *both* sides: `gainers` (sorted by largest positive change) and `losers` (sorted by largest negative change).

parameters.name: timeframe
parameters.description: Window over which to compute price change.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Per side — you get up to `limit` gainers AND up to `limit` losers.

error.message: Failed to deserialize query string: missing field `timeframe`

get.description: Returns the lowercase snake_case names of every DEX Dexploit currently indexes. These are the values you'll pass as `protocol=` to [/api/v1/pairs](#tag/pairs/get/api/v1/pairs) and [/api/v1/top-movers](#tag/stats-trends/get/api/v1/top-movers), or as `dex=` to [/swaps](#tag/swaps/get/swaps).
Roll-up of the OHLCV index — how many candles, pools, mints, and supported timeframes; plus when the index started.

data_inception.description: When Dexploit started ingesting Solana swaps.

get.description: Fetch `/stats/token/{mint}` for up to 200 mints in one round-trip. Returned object is keyed by mint.

get.summary: LP and burn events for a token
get.description:
Non-swap pool activity for a token: LP deposits, LP withdrawals, and token burns across every indexed pool.

Useful for detecting rug-pulls (large `lp_withdraw` events), tracking dev positions, or surfacing freshly-locked liquidity.

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: Cursor — epoch ms; returns events strictly older than this.

post.summary: Filter tokens by volume / price-change / liquidity / audit / sector
post.description:
Server-side filtered + sorted token list. Filters combine as AND. The screener evaluates against a rolling 1h window.

The NL-style query bar on the Dexploit terminal screener is sugar over this endpoint — the chip bar serializes into the same JSON body.

get.description: Resolve metadata + audit flags for up to 200 mints in one call. This is the right endpoint for hydrating a list view — don't loop over `/tokens/{mint}`.
Metadata + audit for one mint. Includes the Dexploit-hosted `image_url_cached` mirror.
Just the audit subset of `/tokens/{mint}`. Cheaper when you only need rug-check signals.

get.summary: Token intelligence (snipers, insiders, top holders, dev)
get.description:
Aggregated actor classification and price/liquidity context for a single token. Useful for a 'token detail' panel. Server-side cached ~60s.

Classifications come from wallet tagging — see [`/wallets/{wallet}/tags`](#tag/wallet-intel/get/wallets/{wallet}/tags) for the per-wallet view.

get.summary: Per-tag count of holders for a token
get.description: How many wallets currently holding this token fall in each tag bucket. Drives the 'sniper count', 'whale count' badges on token cards.

Returns the set of tags Dexploit's wallet-intel pipeline has applied to a wallet (e.g. `sniper`, `insider`, `whale`, `smart_money`). Tags are computed asynchronously — `computed_at: 0` means we haven't profiled this wallet yet.

Stream of mint-create / bonding-progress / graduation events for pump.fun tokens. Filter by lifecycle status to surface fresh mints (`new`), tokens about to migrate (`graduating`), or completed migrations (`migrated`).

For a real-time push of these events, see [WebSocket: pump lifecycle](/streaming/pump-lifecycle).

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Lifecycle phase. `new` = recent mints; `graduating` = bonding curve near 100%; `migrated` = already on pumpswap.

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: Cursor — epoch seconds; returns events strictly older than this.

next_before_ts.description: Pass this back as `before` for the next page; null when exhausted.

get.description: Returns 200 if the API process is alive. Requires authentication like every other endpoint.
Returns 200 if dependencies (ClickHouse, cache) are healthy; 503 otherwise. Requires authentication.
